brokenLinks.impact: The server returned HTTP 429 for "https://www.instagram.com/geeks_for_geeks/". This is a client-side error — the request was understood but refused. Possible causes: authentication required (401), forbidden resource (403), or method not allowed (405).
brokenLinks.recommendation: Inspect why the server is rejecting the request. If the link requires authentication, ensure it is only shown to authenticated users. If the resource is forbidden, update the link to an accessible alternative. HTTP 429 responses from crawled links can also waste crawl budget.

brokenLinks.impact: The server returned HTTP 403 for "https://twitter.com/geeksforgeeks". This is a client-side error — the request was understood but refused. Possible causes: authentication required (401), forbidden resource (403), or method not allowed (405).
brokenLinks.recommendation: Inspect why the server is rejecting the request. If the link requires authentication, ensure it is only shown to authenticated users. If the resource is forbidden, update the link to an accessible alternative. HTTP 403 responses from crawled links can also waste crawl budget.

brokenLinks.impact: Users who follow this link land on a 404 error page — a dead end that breaks their journey through the site. Search engines that crawl this link waste their crawl budget and may reduce their trust in the site, lowering SEO rankings for pages that link to it.
brokenLinks.recommendation: Remove or update the hyperlink pointing to "https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/tag/machine-learning/". If the target page has moved, replace the URL with the correct destination or set up a 301 redirect from the old URL. Regularly audit outbound links with a link checker.

uiIssues.description: Link 1 (href: "https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/") has no visible text and no aria-label.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers read this link as "link" with no destination hint, so users cannot decide whether to follow it. Search engines treat anchor text as a relevance signal — an empty link passes no keyword value to the linked page.
uiIssues.recommendation: Provide descriptive anchor text (e.g., "View full report") or an aria-label that describes the destination. Avoid generic text like "click here" or "read more" — be specific about where the link goes.

uiIssues.description: Link 48 (href: "https://geeksforgeeksapp.page.link/gfg-app") has no visible text and no aria-label.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers read this link as "link" with no destination hint, so users cannot decide whether to follow it. Search engines treat anchor text as a relevance signal — an empty link passes no keyword value to the linked page.
uiIssues.recommendation: Provide descriptive anchor text (e.g., "View full report") or an aria-label that describes the destination. Avoid generic text like "click here" or "read more" — be specific about where the link goes.

uiIssues.description: Input field 1 (type="text") has no associated <label>, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title attribute (has placeholder "Search..." — placeholder is not a substitute for a label).
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers cannot tell users what to type into this field. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A violation (Success Criterion 1.3.1 and 3.3.2). Unlabelled fields are also harder to tap accurately on mobile because the touch target for the label is missing.
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a <label for="inputId"> element whose for attribute matches the input's id. If a visible label is not desired, use aria-label="Field purpose" on the input itself, or aria-labelledby pointing to an existing descriptive element. Note: placeholder text is not a valid replacement for a label.

uiIssues.description: Input field 2 (type="text") has no associated <label>, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title attribute.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers cannot tell users what to type into this field. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A violation (Success Criterion 1.3.1 and 3.3.2). Unlabelled fields are also harder to tap accurately on mobile because the touch target for the label is missing.
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a <label for="inputId"> element whose for attribute matches the input's id. If a visible label is not desired, use aria-label="Field purpose" on the input itself, or aria-labelledby pointing to an existing descriptive element. Note: placeholder text is not a valid replacement for a label.

uiIssues.description: Image 8 (src: "https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dsa/dsa-tutorial-learn-data-structures-and-algorit") has no alt attribute or an empty one.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers announce this image as "image" with no context, making it meaningless to visually-impaired users. Search engines also use alt text to index images, so missing alt text reduces SEO value.
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute that conveys the purpose or content of the image. For purely decorative images use alt="" (empty string) to tell screen readers to skip it. Avoid generic text like "image" or "photo".

uiIssues.description: Two interactive elements genuinely overlap: a and a. Intersection area: ~3805px² (62% of the smaller element). Detected at 1280×800 resolution.
uiIssues.impact: When clickable elements overlap, users may accidentally trigger the wrong action. On touchscreens the problem is amplified because touch targets are imprecise. This also breaks keyboard tab order, causing confusion for keyboard-only users.
uiIssues.recommendation: Use browser DevTools to identify the overlapping elements and fix their positioning (check for z-index stacking, absolute/fixed positioning, or negative margins). Ensure all interactive elements have a minimum touch target size of 44×44 CSS pixels (WCAG 2.5.5 Level AAA recommendation).

uiIssues.description: Link 230 (href: "https://geeksforgeeksapp.page.link/gfg-app") has no visible text and no aria-label.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers read this link as "link" with no destination hint, so users cannot decide whether to follow it. Search engines treat anchor text as a relevance signal — an empty link passes no keyword value to the linked page.
uiIssues.recommendation: Provide descriptive anchor text (e.g., "View full report") or an aria-label that describes the destination. Avoid generic text like "click here" or "read more" — be specific about where the link goes.

uiIssues.description: Input field 1 (type="text") has no associated <label>, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title attribute (has placeholder "Search..." — placeholder is not a substitute for a label).
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers cannot tell users what to type into this field. This is a WCAG 2.1 Level A violation (Success Criterion 1.3.1 and 3.3.2). Unlabelled fields are also harder to tap accurately on mobile because the touch target for the label is missing.
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a <label for="inputId"> element whose for attribute matches the input's id. If a visible label is not desired, use aria-label="Field purpose" on the input itself, or aria-labelledby pointing to an existing descriptive element. Note: placeholder text is not a valid replacement for a label.

uiIssues.description: Two interactive elements genuinely overlap: a and a. Intersection area: ~3805px² (62% of the smaller element). Detected at 1280×800 resolution.
uiIssues.impact: When clickable elements overlap, users may accidentally trigger the wrong action. On touchscreens the problem is amplified because touch targets are imprecise. This also breaks keyboard tab order, causing confusion for keyboard-only users.
uiIssues.recommendation: Use browser DevTools to identify the overlapping elements and fix their positioning (check for z-index stacking, absolute/fixed positioning, or negative margins). Ensure all interactive elements have a minimum touch target size of 44×44 CSS pixels (WCAG 2.5.5 Level AAA recommendation).

uiIssues.description: Image 13 (src: "https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/data-science/data-science-for-beginners/") has no alt attribute or an empty one.
uiIssues.impact: Screen readers announce this image as "image" with no context, making it meaningless to visually-impaired users. Search engines also use alt text to index images, so missing alt text reduces SEO value.
uiIssues.recommendation: Add a descriptive alt attribute that conveys the purpose or content of the image. For purely decorative images use alt="" (empty string) to tell screen readers to skip it. Avoid generic text like "image" or "photo".
